note: Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Hausdorff.lean:536, docstring "Hausdorff measure on an (e)metric space", def hausdorffMeasure (d : ℝ) : Measure X := mkMetric fun r => r ^ d — this is the d-dimensional Hausdorff measure itself.

Mathlib/Combinatorics/Enumerative/Partition/Basic.lean:57 structure Partition (n) is the type of partitions of n, with a Fintype instance (line 205) so Fintype.card (Nat.Partition n) computes p(n); no decl is literally named as the counting function, and GenFun.lean:26 explicitly marks the p(n)-generating-function identity as "TODO: prove this" — nearby but not a named formalization of the function itself.

Mathlib/NumberTheory/HeckeRing/Defs.lean:189 abbrev HeckeRing (Δ H Z) is the abstract diagonal Hecke ring of a Hecke pair; docstring (lines 24-27) states this is "the Hecke pair underlying the classical Hecke operators T_n" but the file only builds the abstract coset-module/ring framework, not operators acting on modular-form q-expansions — foundational scaffolding, not the classical operator itself.
